summary: Implemented the M7 Beta player app shell: default player mode now shows a top status bar, central map region, selected district inspector, objective/action panel, notices, settings/language entry, and a separated developer overlay. Player mode hides prototype dashboard framing, state hash, revision, milestone workspaces, raw command ids, raw reason codes, and fixture display names by default; the legacy M2-M7 workspaces remain reachable only through explicit debug mode for developer evidence. Shell strings use the accepted i18n foundation for en-US, zh-CN, and system fallback, and React/Pixi remain read-model presentation coordinators without WorldState authority.

tests_run.result: Working tree contains only allowed-path task changes plus this writer handoff.
Task registry listed successfully; M7-APP-SHELL-QUALITY-001 is IN_PROGRESS.
Ready list returned M7-SAVE-MIGRATION-CLOUD-BOUNDARIES-001; current task remains in progress pending review.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M7-APP-SHELL-QUALITY-001

tests_run.result: Writer handoff validates against the project handoff schema.
Vitest web shell suite passed: 1 test file, 8 tests.
Vitest UI suite passed: 1 test file, 15 tests.
Vite Web build completed successfully; existing chunk-size warnings were emitted.
Storybook build completed successfully; Vite emitted existing large-chunk/plugin-timing warnings.
Playwright Chromium suite passed: 15 tests, including player/default shell, language switching, debug-on flows, and viewport overflow checks.
Electron security preferences passed; preload exposes only monsoonDesktop bridge; renderer navigation remains restricted; allowed IPC channels are desktop:getRuntimeInfo and desktop:setFullscreen.
Format, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary checks passed.

decisions.decision: Kept legacy M2-M7 milestone workspaces available only under explicit debug mode instead of deleting them, preserving developer evidence while removing prototype framing from player mode.

The Beta shell derives labels, metrics, objective text, and action availability from existing client read-model slices and existing command factories; it does not own WorldState or duplicate simulation formulas.
Default player-visible district, settlement, route, command status, and objective labels are localized and sanitized away from prototype/test fixture display names.

risks.summary: The Pixi canvas still renders through the existing map-renderer package, which is outside this task's allowed modification scope; deeper map label LOD/art treatment remains for M7-MAP-PRESENTATION-QUALITY-001.

Raw milestone labels, ids, hashes, and reason-code diagnostics are intentionally still present in explicit debug mode for developer validation; QA should verify they are absent from default player mode.

This task touches the web shell consumed by Electron and reads locale through the existing bridge; desktop:security-check passed, but Electron-facing risk should remain visible to security_reviewer after QA routing.

requested_action: Please independently validate M7-APP-SHELL-QUALITY-001 against the task acceptance criteria, especially default player/debug separation, i18n behavior, responsive Storybook/Playwright states, and read-model/command authority boundaries. If Electron shell exposure needs separate signoff, route the security-specific findings onward to security_reviewer.
